header.title: C++ Quiz
header.description: How well do you know C++?

questions.Q: C++ is a multi paradigm language
C++ is a succsessor to C
Of the following, what the type name of a dynamic array in the C++ standard library?
What is a difference between 'std::list' and 'std::vector'
A: std::vector is less optimized for tight memory spaces as opposed to std::list
std::list is not a real type defined in the c++ standard.
std::list is a fixed sized array, where as std::vector is dynamic
std::vector is implemented as a dynamic array, where as std::list is a doubly linked list.

questions.Q: Pre c++23, what was a common way to print to stdout, without importing a C standard header?
You've defined a class named 'Animal' in a seprate header file named 'animal.h', Which of the following is the proper syntax to define the Animal class's constructor in s seperate source file?
What is the proper syntax to define a temple class named 'MyTemplate'?
What is the proper syntax to define a parameter pack?
When is a smart pointer deallocated?
A: When the object that owns the smart pointer is deallocated, or when the scope when the smart pointer was stack allocated on was exited.
Always when the program is terminated.
A Smart pointer determines when to deallocate itself based off the current state of the program.
The C++ garbage collector deallocates smart pointers.

questions.Q: 'delete' and 'new' replace C's stdlib.h functions 'malloc' and 'free'
